message.jsonValidationFailed: JSON validation failed. Check brackets, quotes, and commas.
message.jsonStatePlain: Current content is not valid JSON and will be saved as plain text.
message.lockLoginPasswordPlaceholder: Enter the current account login password
message.lockPasswordMinLength: The lock-screen temporary password must contain at least 4 characters
message.lockTemporaryPasswordUnavailable: No lock-screen temporary password is available.
message.rsaVerifyPublicKeyMissing: Missing frontend RSA public key for response signature verification.
message.browserOnlySupportsAesRequestCrypto: The browser only supports AES request-body encryption.
message.webCryptoUnavailable: The current page URL does not support Web Crypto. Use https://, http://localhost, or http://127.0.0.1 and try again.
message.responseCipherHeaderInvalid: Response X-Cipher metadata is missing or invalid.
message.responseSignMissing: Response is missing its signature marker or sign field.
message.securityFieldCountTooLarge: {0} has too many security fields. Max {1}.
message.securityPayloadTooLarge: {0} exceeds the security processing limit. Max {1} bytes.
message.unsupportedSignatureType: Unsupported signature type: {0}. Only M, A or R is allowed.
message.boundedPaginationInvalid: Invalid paginated response. Loading stopped to avoid using incomplete data.
message.boundedPaginationLimitExceeded: Data exceeds the safe aggregation limit of {0} items or {1} pages.
message.boundedPaginationDuplicate: Paginated results contain duplicate or invalid identifiers. Loading stopped.
message.boundedPaginationIncomplete: Paginated results are incomplete: expected {0} items, received {1}.
message.accessTokenRefreshInvalid: The access-token renewal response is incomplete. Sign in again.
message.rsaKeyPairGeneratedAndDownloaded: RSA key pair generated and downloaded locally in the browser.
message.passwordConfirmMismatch: The two passwords do not match.
message.mfaQrRegenerated: QR code regenerated. Use the new code to finish verification.
message.taskRunNowStateLimited: Only retry, scheduled, or archived tasks can run immediately.
message.cacheSearchLeadingWildcardForbidden: Only exact keys, fixed prefixes, or template searches are supported. Leading wildcards are not allowed.
message.copySecretFailedManual: Copy failed. Select the secret manually and copy it.
message.avatarUploadedSaveToApply: Avatar uploaded. Save to apply it.
message.avatarUploadedRememberSave: Avatar uploaded. Remember to save your profile.
message.latestWorkflowIdFilled: Latest workflowId filled. You can query status now.
message.signWrittenToPlainJson: Sign value written into plain JSON.
message.taskPayloadExampleFilled: Recommended payload example filled for the selected task type.
message.secretIdentifierGenerated: Secret identifier generated. Continue filling file paths.
message.taskWorkflowIdMissing: This task has no workflowId, so workflow status cannot be opened.
message.taskPayloadExampleUnavailable: No recommended payload example for this task type.
message.invalidJsonContent: Current content is not valid JSON and cannot be processed.
message.emptyContentNoop: Current content is empty. Nothing to process.
message.noRetryableTasksOnPage: No retryable tasks on this page. Only retry and dead-letter tasks can be retried in batch.
message.noRetryableFailuresOnPage: No retryable failed events on this page. Only retry and dead-letter events can be retried in batch.
message.signValueUnavailable: No sign value is available to write back. Run signing once or enter the sign value manually.
message.workflowTargetExampleUnavailable: This workflow has no target example.
message.workflowDefaultQueueUnavailable: This workflow has no default queue information.
message.mfaSecretUnavailable: No MFA secret is available to copy.
message.templateKeyMissingForWarmup: No template key was provided, so warmup cannot run.
message.noBuiltInCacheKeysRefresh: No built-in cache keys are available to refresh.
message.noRealCacheKeysExport: No real cache keys are available to export.
message.noRealCacheKeysTtl: No real cache keys are available for TTL lookup.
message.clipboardUnsupported: This browser does not support automatic copy. Please copy manually.
message.currentUserIdMissing: Current user ID does not exist.
message.mfaVerifyContextMissing: Current session lacks MFA verification context. Log in again and retry.
message.mfaBindingContextMissing: Current session lacks MFA binding information. Log in again and retry.
message.accountPermissionReadonly: This account can only view permissions and cannot save role permissions.
message.mfaSelfRebindDenied: MFA is already enabled for this account. Contact an administrator to rebind it.
message.mfaDeviceUnavailable: MFA is enabled for this account, but no usable device is bound. Complete login binding first; if binding still fails, ask an administrator to reset the initial state.
message.accountMfaSecretUnavailable: No MFA secret is available for this account.
message.passwordChangeRequiredGoProfile: This account must change the login password first. Go to Profile to finish it.
message.noDeletableTasksOnPage: No deletable tasks on this page.
message.noRunnableTasksOnPage: No runnable tasks on this page.
message.newUserPasswordRequired: A login password is required for new users.
message.noExportFile: No export file is available to download.
message.signExtractFailedFromDecrypt: Could not extract sign from decrypted result. Fill the signature value manually.
message.cacheLocatorInvalid: Invalid cache locator from the source page. Search again manually.
message.currentMenuPermissionsRefreshFailed: Failed to refresh current session menu permissions. Refresh the page or sign in again.
message.userTagLeaseReleaseRiskTitle: Use only after the original workflow has failed or stopped.
message.userTagLeaseReleaseRiskDesc: Before releasing, confirm no table switch, event dispatch, or compensation task is still running. The API matches the owner exactly by workflowId and mode.
message.latestLeaseReleaseEmptyDesc: No workflow mutex lease has been released yet.
message.userTagWorkflowIdPlaceholder: Enter the workflowId whose lock should be released.
message.userTagLeaseReleaseReasonPlaceholder: Explain that the original workflow has failed or stopped and no table switch, event dispatch, or compensation task is still running.
message.userTagLeaseReleased: User tag workflow mutex lease released.
message.userTagLeaseReleaseConfirmTitle: Release the user tag workflow mutex lease?
message.userTagLeaseReleaseConfirmContent: This lets later user tag workflows acquire execution access again. Confirm the original workflow has failed or stopped and no table switch or event dispatch is still running.
message.userTagLeaseReleaseMfaDescription: Releasing a user tag workflow mutex lease is a production-sensitive operation and requires MFA confirmation.
message.switchSecretStatusMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for switching secret status
message.renewSecretCacheMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for refreshing secret cache
message.confirmRenewSecretCache: This will refresh the AES/RSA cache for secret “{0}”.
message.confirmSecretRuntimeSelfCheck: This will refresh cache and run signing, verification, encryption, and decryption self-checks for secret “{0}”.
message.secretSelfCheckCompleted: Secret self-check completed. Review the result and fix the configuration.
message.adminExportSubmitted: Admin export task submitted. Background processing has started.
message.adminExportCompleted: Admin export completed. The file is ready to download.
message.userExportSubmitted: User export task submitted. Background processing has started.
message.userExportCompleted: User export completed. The file is ready to download.
message.cacheSyncPendingWarning: The database operation completed, but the related cache is not synchronized. Refresh it manually on the cache management page before confirming that the change has taken effect.
message.usePasswordLogin: Use username and password to log in.
message.saveSecretBeforeRuntimeSelfCheck: Save the secret before running runtime self-check.
message.traceIdRequiredForVerify: Original X-Trace-Id is required for signature verification.
message.timestampRequiredForVerify: Original X-Timestamp is required for signature verification.
message.contactAdminResetPassword: Contact an administrator to reset your password.
message.templateCacheSearchPatternRequired: Enter a template cache search pattern.
message.accountResetBeforeFirstLogin: Account reset to the pre-first-login state.
message.configHotReloadStatusQueried: Configuration hot reload status queried successfully.
message.mfaBindAddAccountDesc: Choose manual entry if scanning fails.
message.mfaBindVerifyEnableDesc: Enter the 6-digit code to finish binding.
message.mfaGuideInstallDesc: Install Google Authenticator or Microsoft Authenticator, or use the company-approved TOTP authenticator.
message.mfaGuideScanDesc: Scan the QR code first. If Microsoft Authenticator or another app cannot recognize it, add the account manually and enter the issuer, account, and secret.
message.mfaGuideVerifyDesc: The authenticator generates a 6-digit code every 30 seconds. After binding, login and sensitive operations follow backend MFA policy.
message.mfaMicrosoftScanTip: For Microsoft Authenticator, scan from “Add account -> Other account” first. If it still cannot recognize the QR code, use the manual secret.
message.mfaLoginVerifyDescription: Enter the current 6-digit code from your authenticator to complete login verification.
message.mfaLoginBindForcedDescription: MFA is enforced. This account must bind and enable MFA now, otherwise the next login will be blocked. {0}
message.mfaLoginBindDescription: First login requires MFA device binding. {0}
message.mfaBindCheckFailed: MFA binding verification failed. Check the code and retry.
message.mfaGuideSummaryDesc: If scanning fails, use the manual secret below.
message.mfaSensitiveDefaultDesc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator to continue the sensitive operation.
message.mfaVerifyRequirementsDesc: The code usually refreshes every 30 seconds. Use the latest 6-digit code shown in your authenticator.
message.mfaQrManualFallback: Scan the QR code first. If it fails, use the manual secret below.
message.mfaInputCodeHint: Use the latest 6-digit code shown in your authenticator.
message.mfaBindContinueAlertTitle: Continue this sensitive operation after verification
message.mfaBindContinueAlertDesc: Finish binding before continuing. If the code just refreshed, enter the newest digits.
message.mfaRefreshQrFailed: Failed to refresh QR code. Retry later.
message.mfaSensitiveBindDesc: Complete MFA code verification before continuing. This full-screen layer keeps the underlying form from interfering.
message.mfaSensitiveVerifyDesc: Enter the current 6-digit code from your authenticator to continue this sensitive operation.
message.passwordResetRequiredDesc: This account is using an initial or administrator-reset temporary password. Change it before continuing.
message.mfaBindingInfoMissingDesc: The current session lacks MFA binding information. Sign in again and retry.
message.mfaQrRegeneratedForce: QR code regenerated. Bind and enable MFA with the new QR code, otherwise the next login will be blocked.
message.mfaDisabledForce: MFA disabled. MFA is currently mandatory, so bind and verify again before the next login.
message.mfaDisableConfirmContent: MFA is currently mandatory. This session can continue after disabling, but after signing out you must bind and enable MFA again before logging in.
message.mfaForceBindRequiredTitle: This account must rebind and enable MFA
message.mfaForceBindRequiredDesc: MFA is currently mandatory. After signing out, this account must rebind and enable MFA before continuing.
message.mfaBindRequiredDesc: This account must bind an MFA device and verify one code before continuing.
message.mfaQrManualSwitchTip: If scanning fails, switch to manual secret entry.
message.mfaTotpIntro: TOTP MFA is a time-based one-time verification code that adds an extra identity check beyond the password.
message.mfaOfflineAvailableDesc: When the phone and server clocks are correct, the code can be generated even without network access.
message.mfaScanFailedDesc: Use the manual secret above to enter the issuer, account, and secret to complete binding.
message.passwordMismatch: The two passwords do not match.
message.accountPasswordNoticeDesc: Messages from other users will be sent as in-app messages.
message.systemMessageNoticeDesc: System messages will be sent as in-app messages.
message.todoTaskNoticeDesc: Todo tasks will be sent as in-app messages.
message.securityQuestionUnset: No security question is set. Security questions help protect the account.
message.mfaDeviceUnboundDesc: No MFA device is bound. Bind one to enable secondary confirmation.
message.passwordRuleHelp: Password must be 8-12 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, and digits. Special characters are allowed.
message.passwordRuleMessage: {0} must be 8-12 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, and digits. Special characters are allowed.
message.emptyCopyContent: No content is available to copy.
message.profilePasswordResetRequiredDesc: This is an initial or administrator-reset temporary password. Change it first; MFA can be set later.
message.profileMfaStandaloneTip: MFA binding is recommended on the dedicated page.
message.mfaScanQrCaption: Scan the QR code below with an authenticator.
message.mfaQrRightManualTip: If scanning fails, use the manual secret on the right.
message.mfaManualSecretCopied: MFA manual secret copied to clipboard.
message.mfaManualSecretUnavailable: No MFA manual secret is available to copy.
message.profileMfaEnabledNoMaterial: MFA is enabled for this account. View binding instructions on the dedicated page; to disable it, verify the current code first.
message.profileMfaNoMaterial: No binding material has been generated for this account. Use the dedicated page to complete MFA binding.
message.avatarUploadMissingUrl: Avatar uploaded, but no accessible URL was returned.
message.avatarUploadUnavailable: The backend avatar upload API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.newPasswordEmpty: No new password is available to copy.
message.messageReceiversHelp: Leave empty to broadcast to all enabled administrators.
message.title: Title
message.messageContentPlaceholder: Enter content. Bold, lists, links, and multiple lines are supported.
message.messageLinkPlaceholder: Enter a route (/profile-manage/message) or external URL (https://...).
message.messageExtraDataPlaceholder: Optional JSON text for later navigation or detail display.
message.confirmClearReadMessages: Clear all read messages? This only affects the current account inbox.
message.sendMessageDesc: Supports inbox messages and notifications. Choose receivers, or leave empty to broadcast to all enabled administrators.
message.messageSpecDesc: Message type and level support later filtering, alert linkage, and statistics.
message.messageExtraDataSuggestion: Use JSON for extra data so later navigation and detail views can consume it.
message.securityFrontendConfigDesc: Shows the build-time signature, verification, encryption, and decryption config status. Keys are only shown as configured or not; plaintext is never displayed. See apps/web-antd/.env.security.example for the full variable list.
message.securityPasteParseDesc: Paste browser Network Request/Response or curl text (including -H and --data-raw). The parser detects direction and fills AppID, X-Trace-Id, signature fields, and X-Cipher by route policy when possible.
message.cipherFieldsPlaceholder: Field encryption/decryption config, comma-separated, e.g. password,token or json:user
message.wholeBodyCipherForbidden: Whole-body encryption is disabled. Use field-level encryption instead.
message.payloadJsonPlaceholder: Enter the JSON object text to process.
message.signValuePlaceholder: Enter the signature value to verify.
message.cipherInputPlaceholder: Enter JSON text containing the fields to process.
message.frontendRequestModeDesc: Frontend request mode: signing/encryption runs in the browser, while verification/decryption uses backend debug APIs. Use this for pasted browser request parameters.
message.backendResponseModeDesc: Backend response mode: signing/encryption uses backend debug APIs, while verification/decryption runs in the browser. Use this for pasted API responses.
message.cipherFieldsRequired: Enter encryption/decryption field config in field mode.
message.missingFrontendAesConfig: Missing frontend AES config. Check VITE_ADMIN_SECURITY_AES_KEY / VITE_ADMIN_SECURITY_AES_IV.
message.missingFrontendSecurityAppId: Missing frontend security AppID. Check VITE_ADMIN_SECURITY_APP_ID.
message.missingRequestPrivateKey: Missing frontend request signing private key. Check VITE_ADMIN_SIGNATURE_PRIVATE_KEY.
message.missingResponsePublicKey: Missing backend response verification public key. Check VITE_ADMIN_SIGNATURE_PUBLIC_KEY.
message.frontendRequestOnlyAesEncrypt: The actual frontend request chain only supports AES encryption.
message.frontendResponseOnlyAesDecrypt: The actual frontend response decryption chain only supports AES.
message.responseTraceIdRequiredForVerify: Original X-Trace-Id is required for backend response verification.
message.responseTimestampRequiredForVerify: Original X-Timestamp is required for backend response verification.
message.signValueRequired: Enter the signature value to verify first.
message.roleAssignIndependentDesc: Only descendant roles managed by the current admin can be assigned; own, parent, sibling, other-branch, and disabled roles are not selectable. Selecting a parent role removes selected child roles, and covered child roles are filtered before submit.
message.permissionTreeReadOnlyDesc: This account can only view the permission tree and cannot save changes.
message.permissionTreeUsageDesc: Filter permissions by name, code, or module. Click a parent to toggle only that node; double-click a parent to toggle children. Disabled nodes are display-only and are not saved.
message.permissionTreeUsageSearch: Filter permissions by name, code, or module.
message.permissionTreeSingleClickDesc: a parent to toggle only that node;
message.permissionTreeDoubleClickDesc: a parent to toggle children. Disabled nodes are display-only and are not saved.
message.searchPermissionKeyword: Search permission name, code, or module
message.noSpecialConfigEditor: No dedicated editor is configured for this dictionary item.
message.adminIpWhitelistEditorDesc: Maintain the admin login IP whitelist array on a dedicated page. Saving still writes back to the adminIpWhitelist dictionary value.
message.adminIpWhitelistEditorGuideDesc: Each entry must be one IPv4 or IPv6 address. CIDR, wildcards, and remark text are not supported. You can bulk import text separated by commas, spaces, semicolons, or newlines.
message.adminIpWhitelistBulkDesc: Supports comma, space, semicolon, and newline separated values. Duplicates are removed after import.
message.adminIpWhitelistBulkPlaceholder: Paste IPs separated by commas, spaces, semicolons, or newlines.
message.adminIpWhitelistInvalidCount: {0} invalid IP entries exist. Fix or remove them before saving.
message.adminIpWhitelistCurrentConfigDesc: Saving sends the current dictionary version to avoid overwriting a concurrent change.
message.adminIpWhitelistConfigMissing: adminIpWhitelist dictionary config was not found.
message.adminIpWhitelistLoadFailed: Failed to load IP whitelist config
message.adminIpWhitelistLoadFailedWithReason: Failed to load IP whitelist config: {0}
message.adminIpWhitelistSaveFailed: Failed to save IP whitelist config
message.adminIpWhitelistSaveFailedWithReason: Failed to save IP whitelist config: {0}
message.mfaScenarioEditorDesc: Maintain sensitive operation scenarios that skip MFA second verification with checkboxes. Saving still writes back to the original dictionary value.
message.mfaScenarioEditorGuide: No sensitive operation is skipped by default
message.mfaScenarioEditorGuideDesc: After a scenario is checked, it is written into the adminDisableMFACheckScenario dictionary value. When mandatory MFA is enabled, the backend will no longer require second verification for that scenario. Login is not affected by this config.
message.mfaScenarioCurrentConfigDesc: Saving sends the current dictionary version to avoid overwriting a concurrent change.
message.mfaScenarioConfigMissing: adminDisableMFACheckScenario dictionary config was not found.
message.mfaScenarioLoadFailed: Failed to load MFA scenario config
message.mfaScenarioLoadFailedWithReason: Failed to load MFA scenario config: {0}
message.mfaScenarioSaveFailed: Failed to save MFA scenario config
message.mfaScenarioSaveFailedWithReason: Failed to save MFA scenario config: {0}
message.redisOverviewDesc: Overview data comes from Redis INFO. Click refresh overview on the right to fetch the latest status.
message.cacheSearchPlaceholder: Enter cache keys, wildcards, or comma-separated keys.
message.routeSearchSourceHint: Current result was located from {0}; you can view matching template cache instances and values directly.
message.nonBuiltInCacheReadonly: Non-built-in cache keys only support viewing current Redis values. Refresh is forbidden on this page.
message.onlyRefreshableBuiltInCache: Only built-in refreshable caches can be refreshed
message.templateKeyDrawerDesc: This subpage shows all real keys matched by a template cache and supports viewing or refreshing individual values.
message.templateCacheSearchPlaceholder: Enter a template cache search pattern, e.g. config_uuid:*
message.realKeyFilterPlaceholder: Filter real keys, e.g. :1 or admin:info:
message.taskListConsoleDesc: Focus on task search, failure recovery, batch actions, and workflow troubleshooting. The first screen keeps query and actions up front.
message.currentResultFromSource: Current result was brought in from {0}
message.aggregateQueryViewDesc: Currently querying by {0}. If queue is empty, multiple queues are aggregated. If group is empty, aggregating state is not queried by default.
message.queueAllPlaceholder: Leave empty to query all built-in queues
message.workflowIdFilterPlaceholder: Filter tasks in the same workflow chain
message.taskListOperationHint: Empty state aggregates common states and sorts by task activity time descending. With a time range and no state selected, active, retry, archived, and completed tasks are searched. Fill aggregate group when querying aggregating; scheduled, retry, and archived support Run Now; pending, scheduled, retry, and archived support Delete; scheduled task time range filters by next run time.
message.taskListGridDesc: Focus on current filtered results for recovery runs, deletion, and detail troubleshooting.
message.collectorConsoleDesc: View Kafka publish/consume, Processor batch throughput, task hotspots, failure reasons, and retry strategy in one place. Risky actions limit batch size by default.
message.collectorTimeoutRiskDesc: Retrying events may have timed out. Check Processor logs and downstream MySQL latency before deciding whether batches are too slow or leases too short.
message.collectorBacklogRiskDesc: Many due failed events are waiting. Watch 1-minute throughput, average cost, and dead-letter growth before scaling or tuning Processor batches.
message.collectorDeadRiskDesc: Dead-letter events exist. Check event payloads, consumer logic, and downstream dependencies, then retry in small batches. Use delayed retry when the issue is transient.
message.collectorRetryRiskDesc: Retry events exist. Sample failure reasons and payloads before batch retry. Increase delay seconds for transient spikes.
message.collectorHealthyDesc: Collector failure ledger looks stable. Use the runtime cards to watch recent processing throughput, average cost, and task hotspots.
message.collectorConfirmRunTitle: Run one Collector failure retry cycle?
message.collectorConfirmRunDesc: The backend will run one failure ledger retry cycle immediately. Keep the processing limit controlled to avoid downstream spikes.
message.collectorConfirmRetryDesc: Retry {0} failed events in the current result. Sample failure reasons and payloads before running a batch action.
message.collectorNoRuntimeBizTypeRank: No runtime task rank data yet.
message.collectorNoBizTypeRank: No bizType hot rank data yet.
message.collectorFilterOperationDesc: Filter first, then act. Locate issues by bizType and failure state before batch retry or manual run.
message.collectorMatchedTotalHint: Matched total: {0}. Batch retry only selects retry/dead-letter events and is capped by retry limit.
message.collectorResetAttemptHint: Current retry strategy: reset attempt, subject to backend behavior.
message.secretOnlyAbsolutePathNoPem: Only absolute file paths are allowed. Do not enter PEM content.
message.secretAbsolutePathRequired: Use an absolute path when this field is filled.
message.secretUuidHelp: The AppID decoded from X-App-Id. It must match the frontend environment config.
message.secretUuidLockedPlaceholder: Secret identifier is locked in edit mode.
message.secretUuidPlaceholder: Enter or generate a secret identifier.
message.secretKeyVersionHelp: The version being edited or created. Use another version under the same uuid to add a version.
message.secretSignStatusHelp: Disables signing for this AppID without changing encryption.
message.secretCryptoStatusHelp: Disables encryption for this AppID without changing signing.
message.secretStableVersionHelp: The runtime default version. If empty, it defaults to the current version.
message.secretGrayVersionHelp: The gray version must already exist under this uuid or be submitted now. Same as stable means gray is disabled.
message.secretGrayVersionPlaceholder: Leave empty when gray is disabled.
message.secretGrayPercentHelp: Gray traffic percentage, 0-100. A gray version is required when greater than 0.
message.secretAesKeyRefHelp: Required for encryption/decryption. Only an absolute server AES KEY file path is allowed.
message.secretAesIvRefHelp: Required for encryption/decryption. Only an absolute server AES IV file path is allowed.
message.secretServerPublicKeyHelp: Optional. When empty, it is derived from the server RSA private key. Use only for deployments that require a separate public key file.
message.secretServerPrivateKeyHelp: Required for signing verification or encryption/decryption. Private key content must not appear in the database or frontend.
message.secretUserPublicKeyHelp: Required for signing verification or encryption/decryption. Only an absolute user-side RSA public key file path is allowed.
message.secretEditModeDesc: This uses “main config + version config”. Saving refreshes version routing and material caches.
message.secretAddModeDesc: Plan the uuid and version first, then enter absolute key file paths. Keep it disabled until validation passes.
message.secretEditRiskDesc: Editing an existing secret affects request verification, response signing, and encryption/decryption for this uuid. Confirm stable/gray versions, mounted paths, and rollback before saving.
message.secretTipIdentifierDesc: The identifier is the AppID decoded from the X-App-Id signing/encryption header. One uuid represents one integration app, provider, or channel.
message.secretTipPathModeDesc: Only absolute server file paths are allowed. AES plaintext and RSA PEM text are not accepted. The local RSA tool only generates and downloads PEM in the browser.
message.secretTipDirectoryDesc: Prefer `/etc/admin/keys/<uuid>/<keyVersion>/`. Runtime routing uses stable or gray versions to avoid overwriting production files directly.
message.secretTipEnableCheckDesc: When enabled, the backend checks file existence, readability, AES length, and RSA PEM format immediately. Private key content is never returned to the frontend.
message.secretTipSecuritySwitchDesc: Signing and encryption control separate runtime paths and do not change each other; disabled capabilities do not require their file paths.
message.secretRuntimeSelfCheckCompleted: Secret runtime self-check completed. Fix issues according to the result.
message.secretPathRegistrationDesc: This module supports version switching, path validation, runtime self-check, and browser-local RSA file generation.
message.secretLocalRsaDesc: Generates and downloads `public.pem` and `private.pem` locally in this browser. Nothing is uploaded, saved on this page, or written back to the secret config.
message.secretGenerateDownloadRsa: Generate and download RSA key pair
message.secretHighRiskDesc: The browser-generated RSA private key must be stored offline by operators only, then distributed through K8s Secret or mounted directories. Do not share private key content through chats, screenshots, or tickets.
message.secretValidationResultDesc: Only validation results are shown; file content and internal error chains are not returned.
message.secretSelfCheckMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for secret runtime self-check
message.secretPathValidateMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for secret path validation
message.secretPathValidateCompleted: Secret path validation completed. Fix issues according to the result.
message.secretEditMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for editing secret key
message.secretAddMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for adding secret key
message.hotReloadCurrentStatusDesc: Status parsed from lastStatus, used first to judge whether hot reload is healthy.
message.hotReloadEnabledDesc: enabled=false means config hot reload is disabled and the watcher will not run.
message.hotReloadWatchingDesc: watching=true means the background poller is started. If enabled=true but watching=false, the watcher is likely not running.
message.latestTriggerSourceDesc: Shows whether hot reload was manual, startup initialization, or file-change triggered.
message.configFileDesc: The watched config file path. It should match the -f file used at startup.
message.pollIntervalDesc: Interval for checking config file changes. Smaller values respond faster but check more often.
message.configVersionDesc: Identifies the active config snapshot fingerprint. It usually changes after config updates.
message.restartRequiredDesc: Some components may still require process restart even when hot reload succeeds.
message.restartReasonDesc: When restartRequired=true, this shows the reason summary.
message.latestResultMessageDesc: Raw result description of the latest hot reload. Check here first for failures.
message.latestFailureCategoryDesc: When the latest run failed, this usually categorizes parse, read, or runtime validation failures.
message.suppressedFailureCountDesc: How many times the same failure was rate-limited. Continuous growth means the same issue repeats.
message.latestCheckTimeDesc: The latest time the background process checked config file changes.
message.latestReloadTimeDesc: The latest time hot reload actually ran.
message.latestSuccessTimeDesc: The latest time hot reload completed successfully.
message.latestFailureTimeDesc: Latest hot reload failure time. Empty means no recent failure.
message.noConfigHotReloadPermissionDesc: Ask an administrator to grant hot reload status query, config item view, or manual trigger permission before operating.
message.configHotReloadGuide: Check current status and latest result first. Trigger manual hot reload only after confirming config file changes.
message.apiConfigHotReloadGuide: Query or trigger in-process config hot reload for the API service. Infrastructure config still requires a restart.
message.triggerApiHotReloadMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for triggering API hot reload
message.apiConfigHotReloadStatusQueried: API config hot reload status queried successfully.
message.configRuntimeItemsGuide: After querying, view the runtime.yaml-shaped view, full masked YAML snapshot, and item details currently effective in this process.
message.apiConfigRuntimeItemsGuide: After querying, view the external runtime config currently effective in the API process, the full masked YAML snapshot, and item details.
message.configItemSearchPlaceholder: Search config path or masked display value
message.configTotalItemsDesc: Displayable leaf config count in the current runtime snapshot
message.configSensitiveItemsDesc: Items already handled by backend masking rules
message.configSnapshotVersionDesc: Current config fingerprint from hot reload status
message.queueCriticalDesc: High-priority queue for core workflows and urgent manual triggers.
message.queueDefaultDesc: Default queue for regular tasks and common workflows.
message.queueMaintenanceDesc: Maintenance queue for cleanup, compensation, aggregation, refresh, and other low-priority tasks.
message.queueCustomDesc: Custom queue. Check cron config for its purpose.
message.hotReloadTriggerManualApi: Triggered by the manual hot reload button
message.hotReloadTriggerStartup: Initialized config snapshot at service startup
message.hotReloadTriggerWatcher: Triggered automatically by the watcher after config changes
message.executionTargetsPlaceholder:
Separate by newline or comma. Each item is passed to the backend as a string.
Example: tag=1
mode=full
message.workflowDefaultQueuePlaceholder: Empty means using workflow default queue
message.uniqueKeyPlaceholder: Optional: the same unique key can only trigger once within TTL window to prevent duplicate clicks/triggers.
message.selectRegisteredTaskType: Select or search registered task type
message.taskDefaultQueuePlaceholder: Empty means using the task default queue
message.aggregateGroupTaskPlaceholder: Optional: tasks with the same group may be aggregated or merged, depending on backend policy.
message.guideTargetsDesc: Separate by newline or comma. Each item is passed through as a string and parsed by the workflow. Prefer key=value for audit and replay.
message.guideWorkflowQueueDesc: Empty means using the workflow default queue. Prefer maintenance for compensation/batch tasks; use critical for urgent fixes.
message.guideShardTotalDesc: Splits targets into parallel shards. Higher values increase concurrency and downstream pressure. Leave empty for backend defaults.
message.guideGrayPercentDesc: Controls hit ratio from 1 to 100. For example, 10 means about 10% of targets, useful for gray validation.
message.guideUniqueDesc: Prevents duplicate clicks or triggers. The same uniqueKey can only trigger once within the TTL window. Leave empty if not needed.
message.guideRetryTimeoutDesc: Overrides backend retry and timeout defaults. Set explicitly for production compensation to avoid long-tail resource usage.
message.guideProcessTimeDesc: Choose one: processAt is an RFC3339 absolute time, processInSeconds is relative delay seconds. Leave both empty for immediate execution.
message.guideDeadlineDesc: Declares the latest executable time. Tasks past it are usually discarded or failed to prevent expired execution.
message.guideTaskTypeDesc: Select a registered task type. Prefer tasks marked as recommended for manual enqueue to avoid internal node tasks.
message.guideTaskPayloadDesc: Must be valid JSON. Fill the recommended example first, then adjust fields. JSON is passed to the backend task handler as-is.
message.guideTaskQueueDesc: Empty means using the task type default queue. Prefer maintenance for compensation; use default/critical according to queue responsibility.
message.guideGroupDesc: Optional aggregation or merge group. Backend policy decides whether and how it takes effect.
message.guideTaskRetryTimeoutDesc: Overrides backend retry and timeout defaults. Set explicitly for production compensation.
message.guideTaskProcessTimeDesc: Choose one: processAt is an RFC3339 absolute time, processInSeconds is relative delay seconds. Leave both empty for immediate enqueue.
message.guideTaskDeadlineDesc: Declares the latest executable time. Expired tasks are usually discarded or failed, useful for time-sensitive tasks.
message.guideUniqueWindowDesc: Controls dedupe or merge within a time window. Leave empty if not needed.
message.visibleQueuesDesc: Number of executable queues visible to the current task system
message.registeredTaskTypesDesc: Number of task types registered and recognized by the current process
message.registeredWorkflowsDesc: Number of workflows registered by the current process
message.schedulerStatusNotLoaded: Scheduler runtime status not loaded yet
message.schedulerCurrentStatusDesc: Status parsed from lastStatus, used first to judge whether scheduler is running normally.
message.schedulerConfigSwitchDesc: enabled=false means periodic scheduler is explicitly disabled in config and this process will not schedule.
message.leaderElectionDesc: running=true means this process started the leader competition loop, not necessarily that it holds leader.
message.currentProcessNoLeader: Current process does not hold leader
message.leaderHeldDesc: hasLeader=true means this process handles actual periodic scheduling. Not holding leader can be normal in multi-instance deployments.
message.validPeriodicTaskCountDesc: Number of valid periodic tasks participating in scheduling. 0 usually means no effective periodic config.
message.instanceIdDesc: Used to map deployment instances, logs, or operation panel node identifiers.
message.leaderLockKeyDesc: Redis key used for scheduler leader competition. Check this first for multi-instance competition issues.
message.latestSyncResultDesc: Periodic task config sync result. If failed, check sync message and backend logs.
message.latestSyncMessageDesc: Raw message of latest sync result, used to locate periodic config parse or load issues.
message.latestEnqueuedTaskDesc: Shows the latest periodic task name/type enqueued by scheduler, used to judge if scheduling is progressing.
message.latestEnqueueFailureDesc: If the latest periodic enqueue failed, check the reason here first.
message.latestOverallMessageDesc: Latest scheduler overall status message, useful for quick triage with current status.
message.leaderLeaseDesc: Leader lock TTL. Too short may cause frequent switching; too long slows recovery.
message.renewIntervalDesc: Interval for current process to renew leader lock. It should be clearly shorter than the lease TTL.
message.syncIntervalDesc: Interval for scheduler to re-sync periodic config and detect config changes.
message.heartbeatIntervalDesc: Interval for scheduler leader heartbeat reporting, used to check if the leader is stable.
message.latestStartTimeDesc: The latest time the leader competition loop started.
message.latestHeartbeatTimeDesc: Latest scheduler heartbeat time. If it does not update for a long time, scheduling may be stuck.
message.latestAcquireLeaderDesc: The latest time current process acquired leader successfully.
message.latestReleaseLeaderDesc: The latest time leader was released or lost. Frequent changes usually indicate competition or stability issues.
message.latestSyncTimeDesc: The latest time periodic config was synced.
message.latestEnqueueTimeDesc: The latest time a periodic task was successfully enqueued.
message.latestEnqueueFailureTimeDesc: The latest time a periodic task enqueue failed. Check failure reason when present.
message.schedulerNotLoadedDesc: Scheduler snapshot has not been read from task queue API. Confirm /api/tasks/queues is accessible.
message.schedulerDisabledGuideDesc: Scheduler is explicitly disabled in backend config. If periodic tasks are expected, check task_queue.scheduler.enabled and deployment parameters.
message.schedulerAbnormalGuideDesc: Scheduler is running, but the latest sync or enqueue has an exception. Check latest sync message, latest enqueue failure, and backend logs first.
message.schedulerNotStartedGuideDesc: Scheduler is enabled, but this process has not started the leader competition loop. Check run_mode, deployment role, and periodic task config.
message.schedulerNoLeaderGuideDesc: Current process joined leader competition but does not hold leader yet. This is usually normal in multi-instance deployments. If no instance has leader, check Redis lock competition and network connectivity.
message.schedulerHealthyGuideDesc: Current process holds leader and no recent sync or enqueue exception is detected. Periodic scheduling looks healthy.
message.confirmInternalTaskEnqueueDesc: Task type [{0}] is marked as not recommended for manual enqueue. It is usually used by workflow internal nodes or aggregation chains. Continue only after confirming payload structure, timing, and idempotency impact.
message.schedulerNoLatestEnqueuedTaskName: Scheduler has no recent enqueued task name.
message.taskConsoleDesc: Handle task scheduling, workflow triggering, and generic task enqueueing in one place. The first screen keeps real actions up front.
message.workflowTriggerAdvice: Workflow trigger advice: choose a queue first; use either processAt or delay seconds; leave time empty for immediate execution.
message.workflowTriggerGuideDesc: Prefer registered workflows and default queues. For immediate trigger, keep time fields empty. For delayed execution, use one time strategy to avoid processAt and delay seconds confusion.
message.enqueueTaskAdvice: Enqueue advice: task type must be registered, payload must be valid JSON, and time fields can stay empty for immediate enqueue.
message.enqueueTaskGuideDesc: Prefer registered task types to avoid manually enqueuing internal node tasks. Fill the recommended payload example first, then adjust fields.
message.payloadExampleAvailable: ; recommended payload example can be filled with one click.
message.queueTotalDesc: Total visible queues in task center
message.pausedQueueDesc: Number of queues whose consuming is paused
message.pendingTaskTotalDesc: Total pending tasks across all queues
message.activeTaskTotalDesc: Total active tasks across all queues
message.pausedQueueDetectedDesc: Some queues are paused. Confirm whether this was intentional operation to avoid mistaking it for backlog or Worker issues.
message.failedQueueDetectedDesc: There are failed tasks in queues. Open the queues with more failures and check retry, archived, and scheduled states first.
message.queueHealthyDesc: Queues are stable. Continue checking Worker snapshots, backlog, and latency metrics for routine inspection.
message.confirmQueueConsumeActionDesc: Are you sure you want to {1} queue [{0}]?
message.taskQueuePanelDesc: View queue pause state, backlog, execution latency, and Worker snapshots in one place. The first screen prioritizes actionable content.
message.taskQueueListDesc: Open task lists directly from queue overview to handle backlog, failures, and paused consumption.
message.onlineWorkerSnapshotDesc: Current online Worker summary is used to check instance count, concurrency, and queue weight config.
message.workflowFailedNodesGuideDesc: Open task list first and check retry, archived, and scheduled tasks in queue {0}. Only these states support Run Now. {1}
message.workflowFailedNodesGuideTitle: Workflow has failed nodes or failure reason
message.workflowFailedGuideDesc: Workflow reached failed terminal state but backend returned no node error details. Open task list by workflowId and check retry, archived, and scheduled tasks.
message.workflowRunningGuideDesc: Workflow is still running. Continue refreshing status. If it stalls for a long time, open task list and check active, retry, and scheduled states.
message.workflowCompletedGuideDesc: Workflow is completed. To verify the execution chain, open the task list and check entry task and final state of each node queue.
message.noWorkflowReceiptToCopy: No workflow receipt is available to copy.
message.workflowIdFromSource: Current workflowId was brought in from {0}
message.workflowTopologyDesc: Nodes are grouped by dependsOn stages; nodes in the same stage can run in parallel.
message.userTagModeDeltaDesc: Run incremental tag updates for daily sync and continuous catch-up.
message.userTagModeDeltaHint: Usually used for routine production sync. Incremental source is identified by backend fact tables.
message.userTagModeFullDesc: Rebuild tags for the target scope, suitable for large repairs or initialization.
message.userTagModeFullHint: Set shard count, batch size, and worker count first to avoid sudden pressure.
message.userTagModeRecalculateDesc: Trigger tag recalculation through workflow chain, suitable for full context and troubleshooting.
message.userTagModeRecalculateHint: Suitable for production compensation. Keep uniqueKey and timeout configured.
message.userTagModeTargetedDesc: Recalculate tags for specified UIDs only, suitable for ticket troubleshooting and gray validation.
message.userTagModeTargetedHint: Fill UIDs first. Add tagTypes only when narrowing recalculation scope is needed.
message.noUserTagWorkflowTriggered: No user tag workflow triggered yet
message.userTagGuideModeDesc: full fits full repair/init, delta fits daily catch-up, targeted fits UID troubleshooting, recalculate fits full recalculation chain.
message.userTagGuideTagTypesDesc: Separate by newline or comma. Empty means backend default scope for current mode. Validate with a small scope first.
message.userTagGuideUidsDesc: Only for targeted mode. Separate by newline or comma. Validate a small UID set first, then expand gradually.
message.userTagGuideScaleDesc: Controls parallelism and batch scale. Higher values improve throughput but increase downstream pressure. Start small for production compensation.
message.userTagGuideDryRunDesc: Only skeleton validation is available. dryRun is fixed on and does not write data; formal execution stays unavailable until business stages are complete.
message.userTagGuideUniqueDesc: Prevents duplicate triggers. The same uniqueKey can only trigger once within TTL window. Set explicitly for production compensation.
message.userTagGuideTimeoutDesc: Overrides backend default timeout. Leave empty for default. Set a reasonable timeout based on scale and downstream stability.
message.userTagRecalculateTagTypesDesc: Required. Separate by newline or comma. Validate a small tag scope first, then expand to avoid resource jitter.
message.userTagRecalculateQueueScaleDesc: Prefer maintenance queue and explicitly set shardTotal / batchSize / workerCount to control execution pace.
message.userTagRecalculateDryRunDesc: Only skeleton validation is available. dry_run is fixed to true; formal recalculation stays unavailable until business stages are complete.
message.userTagRecalculateUniqueTimeoutDesc: uniqueTTLSeconds controls dedupe window. timeoutSeconds controls single task timeout. Set explicitly for production compensation.
message.userTagOpsDesc: Validate user tag workflows, recalculation, and receipts without running formal business writes.
message.userTagSkeletonValidationTitle: Only dry-run skeleton validation is available
message.userTagSkeletonValidationDesc: User tag business stages are not complete. Workflow and recalculation requests are forced to dryRun/dry_run=true until the backend business stages are closed.
message.userTagWorkflowFullScopeInvalid: full mode cannot include UIDs or tag types. Use targeted/recalculate instead.
message.userTagWorkflowDeltaScopeInvalid: delta mode does not support manually specified UIDs or tag types.
message.userTagWorkflowTargetedRequired: targeted mode requires both UIDs and tag types.
message.userTagRecalculateAdvice: Recalculation advice: prefer maintenance queue and control batch size and worker count by tag scope.
message.userTagRecalculateAdviceDesc: Use this to validate the recalculation skeleton. Keep tag scope and execution scale small; no formal business write is performed.
message.userTagProductionAdviceScale: Use maintenance queue for full or recalculation tasks, and set shardTotal, batchSize, and workerCount explicitly.
message.userTagProductionAdviceTargeted: targeted mode is better for ticket troubleshooting. Validate a small UID set first, then expand.
message.userTagProductionAdviceUnique: Keep uniqueKey or uniqueTTLSeconds for production compensation to avoid duplicate triggers.
message.adminPasswordHelp: Required when creating a user. Leave empty while editing to keep the password unchanged. Password must be 8-12 characters and include uppercase letters, lowercase letters, and numbers. Special characters are allowed.
message.parentConfigHelp: Select a parent config. Select Root config for top-level config.
message.dictValueHelp: Supports JSON literals, objects, arrays, booleans, and numbers. Non-JSON content is saved as a plain string.
message.filterByEmailExact: Enter full email for exact search
message.filterByPhoneExact: Enter full phone for exact search
message.userContactFilterExclusive: Choose either email or phone as the search field
message.userStatusFilterUnavailableInShardMode: Status filtering is unavailable while physical user sharding is active
message.permissionUuidHelp: Permission identifier controls frontend menu/page/button visibility. Permission module is used for backend API authorization. Maintain them separately.
message.parentPermissionHelp: Select a parent permission. Select Root permission for top-level permission.
message.parentRoleHelp: Select an existing parent role. Virtual root nodes are no longer shown.
message.activeTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to watch running tasks. If runtime is unusually long, check workflow status and queue load together.
message.aggregatingTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to inspect grouped aggregation tasks. Confirm the group and compare with the maintenance queue.
message.allTaskGuideDesc: Results may contain multiple states. Check failed info and runnable tasks first, then open details.
message.archivedTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to handle archived failed tasks. Check the error and payload before manual recovery.
message.archivedTasksQuickDesc: For final failures or tasks that stopped retrying
message.batchTaskOperationPartialDesc: {0} succeeded and {1} failed. Handle the failed tasks first.
message.commonStatesQuickDesc: Aggregate common states and sort by task time descending
message.completedTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to confirm execution results. If tracing failures, switch back to retry or archived tasks.
message.confirmBatchDeleteCurrentPage: Confirm batch delete for current page
message.confirmBatchDeleteCurrentPageContent: {0} tasks on this page can be deleted. This cannot be undone. Continue?
message.confirmBatchRunCurrentPage: Confirm batch run now for current page
message.confirmBatchRunCurrentPageContent: {0} tasks on this page can run now. Continue?
message.confirmDeleteTaskContent: Are you sure you want to delete task {0}?
message.confirmRunTaskNowContent: Are you sure you want to run task {0} now?
message.deletableTaskCountDesc: Tasks on this page that can be deleted directly
message.failedTaskCountDesc: Tasks on this page with failure information
message.failedTaskIdsCopied: Failed task IDs copied to clipboard.
message.failedTaskQueuePairsCopied: Failed task queue/task IDs copied to clipboard.
message.hitStatusDesc: Actual task state returned by the current query
message.noFailedTaskIdsToCopy: No failed task IDs to copy on this page.
message.noFailedTaskQueuePairsToCopy: No failed task list to copy on this page.
message.pendingTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to check backlog. If pending tasks do not move, check queue pause state, worker status, and backlog.
message.pendingTasksQuickDesc: For checking backlog and consumption blocks
message.queueNameHelp: Supports `critical`, `default`, and `maintenance`. Empty means aggregate built-in queues.
message.retryTaskGuideDesc: Use this view for failure recovery. Check the error first, then run now after dependencies recover.
message.retryTasksQuickDesc: For failure recovery and manual reruns
message.runnableTaskCountDesc: Tasks on this page that can be run for recovery
message.scheduledTaskGuideDesc: Use this view to adjust scheduled tasks. Run now if needed, or delete expired plans.
message.scheduledTasksQuickDesc: For early execution or cancelling scheduled tasks
message.taskDeleteStateLimited: Only pending, scheduled, retry, or archived tasks can be deleted.
message.taskDetailActiveDesc: This task is running. If runtime is unusually long, check workflow status, worker nodes, and queue backlog.
message.taskDetailArchivedDesc: This task is archived, usually because it failed and will not retry automatically. Check the error before recovery.
message.taskDetailCompletedDesc: This task is completed. If tracing failures, keep checking retry or archived tasks in the same workflow or queue.
message.taskDetailDefaultDesc: Use task state, latest error, queue, and workflow information to decide the next action.
message.taskDetailPendingDesc: This task is waiting for a worker. If it stays pending, check queue pause state, worker status, and backlog.
message.taskDetailRetryDesc: This task is in the retry queue. Check the latest error and payload first; rerun early after dependencies recover.
message.taskDetailScheduledDesc: This task is waiting for its scheduled time. Run now if needed, or delete it if no longer needed.
message.taskIdFilterPlaceholder: Enter full task ID or fragment
message.taskNameFilterHelp: Use this to inspect one periodic task or workflow trigger. The backend matches by display name.
message.taskNoFailureError: No failure error for this task.
message.taskNodeLocatedDesc: Filtered by workflowId={0} and node keyword={1}. Results are limited to node {2} in this workflow.
message.taskResultEmptyGuide:
No result data for this task.
Possible reasons:
1. The task has not completed successfully.
2. The task result is empty.
Note: successful tasks show the result summary here.
message.taskRunNowSucceeded: Task {0} has been set to run immediately.
message.workflowFilteredDesc: Currently filtering by a specified workflow chain
message.workflowLinkedCountDesc: Tasks on this page linked to a workflow chain
message.dictionaryAddModeDesc: Confirm the dictionary key, type, and page scope first. Refresh cache from the list if it must take effect immediately after saving.
message.dictionaryEditModeDesc: Current dictionary content will be filled back. If value type or structure changes, check callers and cache status together.
message.dictionaryExampleTip: Examples show the recommended format so frontend, backend, and operations can understand the config quickly.
message.dictionaryJsonEditorTip: The JSON editor supports format, compress, and validation for complex objects, arrays, and nested configs.
message.dictionaryKeyTip: Use `module.purpose` style keys to avoid duplicates or unclear meanings in the same environment.
message.dictionarySensitiveTip: Avoid entering sensitive plaintext. If changes do not take effect immediately, refresh the related cache.
message.dictionaryValueTip: Dictionary values support plain text and JSON literals. Objects, arrays, booleans, and numbers are saved as JSON structures.
message.dictionaryVersionConflict: Dictionary was modified by someone else. Refresh and retry.
message.permissionModelTipDesc: Permission models are used by the backend to control API route access.
message.permissionParentTipDesc: The parent permission determines the list tree level and is filled automatically when adding a child.
message.permissionTypeTipDesc: Menu or directory permissions can bind menus so the frontend can show or hide them by user permission.
message.permissionUuidTipDesc: The permission identifier is unique and lets the frontend show or hide directories, menus, pages, and buttons.
message.roleDescriptionTipDesc: Role description should include scope, responsibility boundary, and maintenance notes for audit.
message.roleNameTipDesc: Role name is used for permission grouping and role assignment. Use a clear business name.
message.roleParentTipDesc: The parent role determines the role tree level and is filled automatically when adding a child.
message.rolePermissionTipDesc: Role permissions are constrained by this account's assignable scope. Drawer save and permission config modal use the same backend validation.
message.roleStatusTipDesc: After disabling a role, accounts linked to it lose those permissions. The super administrator role is always enabled.
message.userTagTagTypesPlaceholder:
Separate by line breaks or commas, for example: 1,2,3
Leave empty to let the backend use the default range for the current mode.
message.userTagTagTypesSimplePlaceholder: Separate by line breaks or commas, for example: 1,2,3
message.userTagUidsPlaceholder: Used in targeted mode. Separate by line breaks or commas, for example: 10001,10002
message.userTagUniqueKeyPlaceholder: Optional. Prevents duplicate triggers; use execution id + operator + time window.
message.userAddModeDesc: Fill basic profile, initial password, and roles first. After saving, role and status can be adjusted from the list.
message.userEditModeDesc: Current user profile will be filled back. Leave login password empty to keep the old password; role changes are saved together.
message.userListAddModeDesc: Fill basic profile and initial password. After saving, the list refreshes and API profile cache is synchronized.
message.userListEditModeDesc: Current user profile will be filled back. This drawer only updates nickname, contact profile, and avatar; use row actions for status and password changes.
message.userNameTip: Keep login username unique and easy to identify. Avoid frequent changes once referenced by business flows.
message.userListPasswordTip: New users require an initial password. Profile editing does not change the password; use row actions to reset it.
message.userPasswordTip: New users require an initial password. During edit, leaving it empty keeps the old password. Password must be 8-12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and digits.
message.userMfaTip: Account management can switch MFA status. If no device is bound, the next login will require MFA binding first.
message.userManageMfaTip: Add, edit, status switch, and password reset operations require MFA confirmation to avoid accidental sensitive changes.
message.userRemarkTip: Remarks should include role responsibility, account purpose, and handover notes for later audit.
message.avatarUploadNoAccessUrl: Avatar uploaded, but no accessible URL was returned.
message.avatarUploadApiUnavailable: Backend avatar upload API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.nicknamePlaceholder: Leave empty to use the username
message.userDirectManageAlert: Admin writes the user table directly; only API process state such as caches and sessions is synchronized through the internal API.
message.userContactInfoDesc: Email and phone are shown masked only. Search requires the full value and uses encrypted storage plus hash matching.
message.userRuntimeSyncDesc: Profile edits invalidate profile cache; disabling or resetting password also invalidates sessions.
message.apiRuntimeSyncSkipped: API runtime sync was not executed.
message.resetUserPasswordMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for resetting user password
message.syncApiRuntimeMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for syncing API runtime
message.userRuntimeSyncAlert: Manual sync only handles API process caches or sessions. It does not modify user table data.
message.adminExportDownloadApiUnavailable: Backend export download API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.adminExportStatusApiUnavailable: Backend export status API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.userExportDownloadApiUnavailable: Backend user export download API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.userExportStatusApiUnavailable: Backend user export status API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.userExportStatusRefreshFailed: Failed to refresh user export status: {0}
message.exportStatusPollingTimeout: Automatic refresh reached its time limit. Use Refresh progress to continue checking.
message.confirmDeleteUser: This will delete user “{0}”. This cannot be undone.
message.editUserRoleMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for editing user roles
message.resetInitialStateAlert: This will clear the account MFA binding, recent login records, and require the user to sign in with the temporary password and change it first.
message.resetInitialStateMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for resetting first-login state
message.switchUserMfaStatusMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for switching user MFA status
message.switchUserStatusMfaTitle: MFA confirmation for switching user status
message.temporaryPasswordPlaceholder: Enter an 8-12 character temporary strong password
message.confirmDeletePermission: Delete permission “{0}”? This cannot be undone.
message.confirmDeleteRole: Delete role “{0}”? This cannot be undone.
message.deletePermissionDangerDesc: High-risk: this will also delete all child permissions and clear role-permission relations and permission cache.
message.deleteRoleDangerDesc: High-risk: this will also delete all child roles and clear account bindings and role permission cache.
message.disableRoleDangerDesc: High-risk: this will also disable all child roles and related account permissions immediately.
message.disableRoleRecoverDesc: To recover, enable the parent role first, then enable child roles as needed.
message.confirmRenewDictionaryCache: This will refresh the cache data for dictionary “{0}”.
message.dictionaryExportApiUnavailable: Backend export API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.dictionaryOptionsLoadFailed: Failed to load complete config options: {0}
message.dictionaryImportApiUnavailable: Backend import API is unavailable or returned an invalid response.
message.dictionaryImportCompleted: Dictionary import completed: {0} created, {1} updated, {2} skipped.
message.batchRefreshSucceeded: Batch refresh succeeded. Processed {0} real cache keys.
message.cacheExactTargetsIgnored: {0} cache targets do not exist or are not accessible and were ignored.
message.cacheRenewAllAdvice: Prefer refreshing specified caches by business scenario. Refresh all only when cache is broadly abnormal, configs changed widely, or operations explicitly require it.
message.cacheRenewAllDangerDesc: Refreshing all built-in caches invalidates auth, config, secret, session, and other caches together. It may trigger many database and Redis rebuilds and affect online API stability.
message.cacheRenewAllDangerTitle: This is a high-risk operation. Proceed carefully.
message.cacheRouteSearchNoMatch: No matching cache key was found.
message.cacheRouteSearchNoMatchFromSource: Opened cache management from {0}, but no matching cache key was found.
message.cacheSearchDangerHint: The input contains a leading wildcard. The backend will reject it to avoid expensive Redis full scans.
message.cacheSearchDefaultHint: Supports exact keys, fixed prefixes, and registered template search. Prefer exact keys or template prefixes.
message.cacheSearchExactHint: This should use the exact-key fast path and will not trigger Redis SCAN.
message.cacheSearchMultiHint: Multiple search conditions detected. The frontend will query each with controlled concurrency; registered template prefixes use providers and unknown wildcards are rejected.
message.cacheSearchProviderHint: This should hit the template provider fast path, enumerate candidates, then check Redis existence in batch.
message.cacheSearchSummary: Page {0}, {1} per page, {2} real keys confirmed{3}
message.cacheSearchTargetsIgnored: {0} cache targets failed to query and were ignored.
message.cacheSearchUnknownWildcardHint: This is not a registered template wildcard pattern. The backend will reject it to avoid expensive Redis scans.
message.confirmBatchRenewSearchCaches: This will batch refresh {0} built-in cache keys in the current filtered result. Only instances with explicit rebuild support are included.
message.confirmRenewCache: This will refresh data for cache “{0}”.
message.confirmRenewRealCache: This will refresh data for real cache “{0}”.
message.confirmWarmupTemplateCache: This will enumerate all instances by template key and rebuild cache in batch. It may trigger database rebuilds; run during off-peak hours.
message.noRealCacheKeysToCopy: No real cache keys to copy.
message.tableCacheMetricsLoadFailed: Failed to load table cache runtime metrics. Check metric collection on this instance.
message.tableCacheMetricsScopeDesc: This page shows cumulative in-memory values for the current Admin process and resets after restart. Use Prometheus / Grafana for multi-instance aggregation, history, and alerts.
message.tableCacheSourceRisk: Check database and loader when non-zero
message.templateCacheDrawerOpened: This cache key is a template key. The detail page is open, and you can continue {1} {0} real keys.
message.templateCacheNoRealKeyFound: This cache key is a template key. No matching real key was found, so the detail page was opened for further inspection: {0}
message.templateCacheSearchSummary: Pattern: {0}. Page {1} shows {2} keys, {3} real keys confirmed, {4} refreshable on this page{5}.
message.templateRealKeysFound: {0} real template cache keys found. Open the detail page to view and refresh values.
message.ttlOverviewCount: Viewing TTL information for {0} real cache keys.
message.schedulerMaxQueueBacklogDesc: Checks target queue backlog before periodic enqueue. Zero disables backpressure.
message.taskQueueSnapshotLoadFailed: Failed to load the task queue runtime snapshot. Check the queue service and retry.
message.taskDefaultsFilled: Current task type enqueue defaults applied.
message.workflowTopologySearchPlaceholder: Search nodes, queues, status, or dependencies
message.workflowTopologyCollapsedHint: Topology is collapsed. {0} / {1} nodes currently match.
message.workflowTraceNoMatchedNode: No execution trace matches the current node name.
message.workflowExecutionTraceDesc: Aggregates processed counts by workflow, node, and shard.
message.workflowExecutionProgressDesc: Calculates progress from planned, succeeded, failed, skipped, and remaining instances.
message.mfaForceRebindTitle: Rebind and enable MFA for this account
message.mfaForceRebindDesc: MFA is currently enforced. After this account signs out, it must rebind and enable MFA before logging in again.
message.mfaQrScanFailedTip: If scanning fails, switch to manual secret entry.
message.mfaForceDisableDesc: MFA is currently enforced. This session can continue after disabling it, but the account must rebind and enable MFA before logging in again.
